# Metrics First: The Inference Control Plane

Metrics are the control plane for inference engineering. Without them, hardware
and runtime discussions collapse into preference.

## Chapter Job

Define the metrics that let engineers compare hardware, runtimes, model
architectures, and deployment designs.

## Core Metrics

- Time to first token.
- Inter-token latency.
- Time to last token.
- End-to-end product latency.
- Tokens per second per request.
- Aggregate tokens per second.
- Requests per second.
- Queue time.
- Prefill time.
- Decode time.
- KV cache memory.
- Accelerator utilization.
- CPU utilization.
- Network utilization.
- Cost per request.
- Cost per successful task.
- Quality or task-success score.

## Measurement Rule

Every performance claim should name:

- Model.
- Hardware.
- Runtime.
- Precision or quantization.
- Prompt length distribution.
- Output length distribution.
- Batch and concurrency settings.
- Dataset or traffic source.
- Percentile latency, not only averages.

## Artifact

The reader creates a benchmark card that can be reused throughout the book.
